questions.q: For what achievement is Har Gobind Khorana best known?
questions.explanation: Khorana is celebrated for his work in deciphering how the genetic code directs protein synthesis.

questions.q: In which year did Har Gobind Khorana share the Nobel Prize?
questions.explanation: Khorana shared the Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine in 1968.

questions.q: In which category was Khorana's 1968 Nobel Prize awarded?
questions.explanation: He received the 1968 Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine.

questions.q: In which country was Har Gobind Khorana born?
questions.explanation: He was born in Raipur, a village in Punjab in British India, now in Pakistan.

questions.q: Khorana's work centered on which fundamental biological molecules?
questions.explanation: Khorana was a biochemist whose work focused on nucleic acids and the genetic code.

questions.q: The genetic code that Khorana helped decode relates DNA/RNA to the synthesis of what?
questions.explanation: The genetic code specifies how sequences of nucleotides direct the synthesis of proteins.

questions.q: Khorana's scientific discipline is best described as which of the following?
questions.explanation: Khorana was a biochemist who worked on the chemistry of nucleic acids.

questions.q: The basic units of the genetic code, each coding for an amino acid, are called what?
questions.explanation: A codon is a sequence of three nucleotides that codes for a specific amino acid.

questions.q: How many nucleotides make up a single codon in the genetic code?
questions.explanation: Each codon consists of three nucleotides (a triplet) that specify an amino acid.

questions.q: Did Khorana share his 1968 Nobel Prize with other scientists?
options: No, the prize was unshared that year
questions.explanation: He shared the 1968 prize with Robert W. Holley and Marshall W. Nirenberg.

questions.q: With which two scientists did Khorana share the 1968 Nobel Prize?
questions.explanation: Khorana shared the prize with Robert W. Holley and Marshall W. Nirenberg.

questions.q: What was the specific reason cited for Khorana's 1968 Nobel Prize?
options: Interpretation of the genetic code and its function in protein synthesis
Invention of the polymerase chain reaction
questions.explanation: The prize honored their interpretation of the genetic code and its function in protein synthesis.

questions.q: Khorana is widely credited as the first to achieve which landmark synthesis?
questions.explanation: Khorana's team accomplished the first chemical synthesis of an artificial gene in the early 1970s.

questions.q: At which U.S. university did Khorana spend the major part of his later career?
questions.explanation: Khorana was a longtime professor at the Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT).

questions.q: At which institution was Khorana working when he did the Nobel-winning genetic code research?
questions.explanation: Much of his code-deciphering work was done at the University of Wisconsin-Madison.

questions.q: Khorana's contribution to cracking the code relied heavily on which technical strength?
options: Chemical synthesis of defined nucleotide sequences
questions.explanation: Khorana chemically synthesized RNA chains of known, repeating sequences to assign codons to amino acids.

questions.q: Which country did Khorana eventually become a naturalized citizen of?
questions.explanation: Khorana became a naturalized citizen of the United States in 1966.

questions.q: Before settling in the United States, Khorana led a research group at a university in which country?
questions.explanation: He headed an organic chemistry group at the University of British Columbia in Canada in the 1950s.

questions.q: Khorana shared a major U.S. honor for science. Which award did he receive?
questions.explanation: Khorana received the U.S. National Medal of Science in 1987.

questions.q: In which country did Khorana earn his PhD in organic chemistry?
questions.explanation: Khorana earned his PhD at the University of Liverpool in England in 1948.

questions.q: The gene Khorana's team first synthesized in the lab coded for which type of molecule?
questions.explanation: His team chemically synthesized the gene for an alanine transfer RNA from yeast.

questions.q: Khorana's experiments used repeating synthetic polynucleotides to determine what?
options: The speed of light in cells
Which codons specify which amino acids
questions.explanation: By making RNA with known repeating sequences, he showed which codons specify which amino acids.

questions.q: Khorana's PhD postdoctoral work in Switzerland was in the laboratory associated with which area?
questions.explanation: He did postdoctoral research with Vladimir Prelog at the ETH (Federal Institute of Technology) in Zurich.

questions.q: In addition to deciphering codons, Khorana's work clarified which feature of the genetic code?
options: That the code is read in non-overlapping triplets from a fixed starting point
That codons are read four bases at a time
That the code differs in every human
That proteins are made of nucleotides
questions.explanation: His experiments confirmed that the code is read in successive, non-overlapping triplets from a fixed point.

questions.q: Khorana's later research at MIT shifted toward studying which membrane protein involved in vision?
questions.explanation: In his later career Khorana studied rhodopsin and other membrane proteins involved in signal transduction.

questions.q: Which class of enzyme, central to joining DNA fragments, was important in Khorana's gene-synthesis strategy?
questions.explanation: DNA ligase was used to join the chemically synthesized DNA segments into a complete gene.

questions.q: In approximately which year did Khorana's team report a synthetic gene that could function inside a living cell?
questions.explanation: Around 1976 his team produced a fully functional synthetic gene that worked inside a bacterial cell.

questions.q: Khorana shared the 1968 Louisa Gross Horwitz Prize. With whom did he share that award?
questions.explanation: Khorana and Marshall Nirenberg shared the Columbia University Louisa Gross Horwitz Prize in 1968.

questions.q: Which statement about Khorana's early education is correct?
options: He earned his master's degree from Punjab University in Lahore
He completed all of his schooling in the United States
questions.explanation: Khorana earned his BSc and MSc from Punjab University in Lahore before studying abroad.

questions.q: An annual symposium and a scholarship program established in India in Khorana's honor primarily support what?
options: Student research exchange in the life sciences between India and the U.S.
questions.explanation: The Khorana Program supports undergraduate research exchange in the life sciences between India and the United States.
